summary: Frontend-only re-test after switch from procedural Three.js Pi to user-supplied GLB at /models/raspberry_pi_3.glb. All 14 review items pass: GLB fetched 200, WebGL canvas mounts 1920x1080, hero title visible, 3 panels rendered, all 9 hotspots present and visible, hotspot-cpu opens detail-cpu containing 'BCM2837', GPIO/USB hotspots open their details, close button removes detail panel, switching hotspots replaces previous detail (GPIO->USB removed prior), OrbitControls drag executes without errors, no JS runtime errors, no console errors, no webpack compile overlay. Visual screenshot confirms the actual Raspberry Pi 3 board with BCM2837 chip, GPIO pins, USB stack, HDMI, Ethernet, microSD all rendering correctly with cinematic lighting.

issues: 'Made with Emergent' badge slightly overlaps the last (Network/Pi-hole) implementation card; same as iteration_1, non-blocking

critical_code_review_comments: RaspberryPi.jsx topY constant (0.35) is hard-coded instead of derived from the GLB bounding box; if the model thickness ever changes, hotspot Y offsets will need re-tuning. Consider deriving topY from box.max.y * modelScale.
useGLTF.preload runs at module scope but the model URL is re-derived on every render from a constant - acceptable but worth noting for any future dynamic model swap.

Auto-rotate (autoRotateSpeed=0.6) plus drei <Html> hotspots can cause the visible button position to oscillate; clicks succeeded in tests via force=true, but real users dragging will briefly miss small targets — consider pausing autoRotate after first user interaction or on hover.
Three.js / @react-three internals still emit deprecation warnings (THREE.Clock, PCFSoftShadowMap). Non-blocking, library-internal.

test_results_detail.panels_visible: PASS - panel-specs, panel-history, panel-implementations all visible
test_results_detail.9_hotspots_present_visible: PASS - cpu, gpio, usb, ethernet, hdmi, power, microsd, wifi, audio (all count=1, visible=true)
test_results_detail.switching_hotspots_replaces_previous: PASS - clicking USB after GPIO removed detail-gpio and showed detail-usb
test_results_detail.orbit_controls_drag_no_errors: PASS - mouse drag on canvas, no thrown errors, canvas still 1920x1080

context_for_next_testing_agent: GLB is served from /app/frontend/public/models/raspberry_pi_3.glb and fetched at app URL /models/raspberry_pi_3.glb (200, cached). After page.goto, wait ~8-9s for the GLB to download+parse before querying hotspots. Hotspots are drei <Html> wrappers; use force=true clicks. Auto-rotate is on (speed 0.6), so re-locate hotspots between retries. Detail panel uses data-testid='detail-{key}' and close button data-testid='detail-close-btn'.
rca of the issue: No issues found. Switch from procedural model to GLB asset is fully functional; auto-center/auto-scale logic in RaspberryPi.jsx correctly normalizes the model so the original camera, hotspot positions, and shadow plane all line up.
